title: Application Tool
intro: Welcome to the tool that will help you create an application (and guide you through the procedure) to change the gender marker on your ID card.
start_over_hint: Starting over means your current progress will be lost. We recommend downloading a backup file.
intro_info: If you want to learn more about the whole procedure, visit the article:
guide_title: How to change the gender marker in documents? A court guide
step_1_desc: Select which documents you plan to attach. The more, the better – it increases the chances of faster processing. Some are always required and cannot be unchecked.
q_0_1_desc: If you haven’t changed your name at the civil registry yet, you can apply for it together with the gender marker change. If the court approves, it will significantly speed up issuing new documents.
q_0_2_desc: Useful if your surname has a gendered ending.
q_0_3_desc: Submitting the application requires a 100 PLN court fee. You may apply for exemption, but it will extend the procedure and require additional forms. Costs that may arise during proceedings (e.g. expert opinions) can be addressed later.
q_1_0_desc: You can obtain it from any civil registry office or via the ePUAP platform.
q_1_1: Proof of court fee payment (100 PLN)
q_1_1_desc: The fee can be paid via bank transfer, at the court cashier, or by purchasing a court fee stamp:
q_1_2_desc: Select opinions you have and diagnoses included
q_1_3_desc: Select certificates you have and diagnoses included
q_1_5_desc: Many judges lack sufficient knowledge about transgender issues, so it is helpful to guide them with an appropriate document. Download here:
q_1_6: Publication “Proceedings in gender recognition cases. A guide” by the Ombudsman
q_1_6_desc: A large document, but worth attaching. You can reduce printing costs by fitting 4 pages per sheet (double-sided). Make sure it's the latest edition (2024).
q_1_7_desc: If you have already changed your name, include the decision as evidence. Alternatively, attach a full birth certificate showing the change.
q_1_8: Documents proving functioning in society as your gender
q_1_8_desc: Evidence of consistent identification – e.g. social media screenshots, workplace ID, university system printouts.
q_2_0_invalid_0: PESEL number must be 11 digits long
q_2_0_invalid_1: PESEL number must contain only digits
q_2_0_invalid_2: The number you entered is not a valid PESEL
q_2_1_desc: Provide the address where you actually live and can receive mail. It does not have to be your registered address.
q_2_2_desc: If you cannot receive correspondence yourself, you can appoint someone to do it for you.
q_2_3_subheading: Choose the district court for your place of residence. If unsure, contact the court’s information office.
q_2_4: I want to provide contact details
q_2_4_desc: Not required, but may facilitate communication
q_2_4_0_invalid_0: This is not a valid e-mail address
q_2_7_invalid_0: The date must be in the past
q_2_7_invalid_1: It seems like you are under 18 – document you generate here applies only to adults
q_2_8_desc: Location depends on document type (full vs short extract).
q_3_0: Briefly describe the beginning of your transgender experience
q_3_0_desc: Example: Around the age of […] I began to feel a discrepancy between my assigned gender and my gender identity, which led to dysphoria and lowered mood.
q_3_1: Since when have you been undergoing hormone therapy?
q_3_2_desc: If your psychologist uses an informed consent model, you may not have undergone formal assessment.
q_3_3: I want to disclose the name I use daily
q_3_4: In social life I function according to my gender identity
